title: Chain link using a pressed-in member
abstract: A chain assembly is provided that includes at least one integrally cast link and at least once fabricated link that comprises two link members that are press fit together. At least one of the link members may include one projection and the other link member may include two projections that are positioned diagonally from each other from one link member to another link member. The projections may be configured to act as interlocking features.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A chain link subassembly (200) having an enclosed perimeter, the subassembly (200) comprising: a main link member (202) that defines the majority of the perimeter of the subassembly (200) and that includes at least one interlocking feature (222, 226, 228); and a press-in link member (204) that defi nes a minority of the perimeter of the subassembly (200) and includes at least one interlocking feature (242, 244, 248) that is at least partially complimentary shaped to the interlocking feature (222, 226, 228) of the main link member (202), wherein the corresponding interlocking features of both members define a plurality of surfaces and at least two of those surfaces (P) are configured to create interference with each other during a pressing operation. 2. The chain link subassembly (200) of claim 1, wherein the main link member (202) further comprises two opposing C-shaped end portions (208) and a straight portion (2 4) that connects the two C-shaped end portions (208), and two bridge portions (220) that extend from opposite C-shaped end portions (208) that define a gap (206) between the bridge portions (220), wherein each bridge portion (220) includes a plurality of interlocking features (222, 226, 228). 3. The chain link subassembly (200) of claim 2 wherein the main link member (202) includes a partial strut (2 2) that extends from the straight portion (214) in a direction toward the gap (206), 4. The chain link subassembly (200) of claim 3, wherein the partial strut (212) defines a pilot feature (234). 5. The chain link subassembly (200) of claim 1, wherein the press-in link (204) member comprise a general T-section configuration that includes a main body portion (240) and a plurality of interlocking features (242, 244, 248) on either side of the main body portion (240). 6. The chain link subassembly (200) of claim 5, wherein the press-in link member (204) further comprises a partial strut (216) that extends from the main body portion (240). 7. The chain link subassembly (200) of claim 6, wherein the partial strut (216) of the press-in link member (204) further includes a pilot feature (236), 8. The chain link subassembly (200) of claims 2 or 5 wherein the plurality of interlocking features (222, 226, 228), (242, 244, 248) includes either a plurality of recesses or projections on each member. 9. A chain assembly (300) comprising: at least one integrally cast link (106); and at least one fabricated link (200) that includes two link members (202, 204) pressed fit together, wherein at least one link member (202, 204) includes one projection (242, 244, 228) and the other link member (202, 204) includes two projections (242, 244, 228) and the projections are positioned diagonally from each other from one link member to the other link member and are configured to act as interlocking features. 10. The chain assembly of claim 9 wherein the fabricated link defines a load vector (L) and the projections (242, 244, 228) define a plurality of surfaces (P) that are substantially perpendicular to the load vector (L) and that are configured to create areas of interference.
